bullets.text: Major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed over 920 people, with international rescue teams arriving to search for hundreds still feared trapped under rubble. The death toll continues to rise, and families are keeping vigil at affected buildings.
The US has struck Iran after an attack on a cargo ship, with Iran accusing the US of violating their deal and responding with strikes on targets linked to American forces.
Burkina Faso has severed diplomatic ties with France, with the West African country's junta accusing its former colonial power of undermining its national interests.
The South Australian town of Naracoorte is embracing migrants, who have helped to enrich its social fabric and boost the economy, despite a housing shortage.
South Australia's energy grid has been pushed to its limits due to record low winds, testing its reliance on renewable energy.
An Australian man has been arrested in Thailand in connection with the discovery of a 17-year-old's body in a suitcase.
Apple is reportedly seeking approval from the Trump administration to purchase memory chips from a blacklisted Chinese company, aiming to alleviate pressure from rising semiconductor prices.
German carmakers are implementing significant job cuts as they face increasing competition from Chinese rivals, posing a threat to Europe's largest economy's industrial model.
The European Central Bank's Isabel Schnabel warned that inflation risks remain, despite a recent US-Iran peace deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
